payload.hypothesis: Map the forced-delta curve upward from the anchor at 8 by testing forced_delta_count=10 with all other levers frozen, to see if adding two more delta features improves holdout R² beyond the current 2.45%.
payload.planner_reasoning: Only one run exists (forced_delta_count=8, R²=2.45%). Stage 1 discovery requires sweeping forced_delta_count across {4,6,8,10,12,14,16} at max_features=80. Testing 10 next follows the 'anchor plus neighbor' rule and moves the search upward; if 10 beats 8 we continue upward, if not we pivot to 6 and below. All other knobs stay frozen to keep comparisons apples-to-apples.

payload.hypothesis: Test forced_delta_count=6 to map the downward side of the forced-delta curve around the current anchor at 8, since moving up to 10 slightly degraded holdout R² (2.45% → 2.33%), suggesting the peak may lie at or below 8.
payload.planner_reasoning: The only completed delta-count sweep points are 8 (best, R²=2.447%) and 10 (worse, R²=2.331%). The degradation when going from 8→10 hints that the optimal region is at or below 8. Testing 6 completes the first local neighborhood around the anchor and determines whether the peak is exactly at 8 or shifts lower. This is a pure Stage-1 discovery move — one lever (forced_delta_count), all other config frozen — and does not repeat any tried fingerprint.

payload.hypothesis: Continue Stage 1 forced-delta mapping at forced_delta_count=12 to confirm the downward trend observed from 8→10 (R² dropped from 2.45% to 2.33%), establishing whether the peak is firmly at 8 before moving to Stage 2 local refinement.
payload.planner_reasoning: Three runs completed in Stage 1 at delta counts {6, 8, 10}. The holdout R² curve shows 8 as the clear peak (2.447%), with 6 at 2.396% and 10 at 2.331%. The asymmetric drop—steeper going up from 8→10 than down from 8→6—suggests the optimum is at or very near 8. Testing 12 confirms the downward trend on the upper side and completes enough of the curve to justify moving to Stage 2 (max_features variation around the winner) if the degradation continues as expected.

payload.hypothesis: The forced-delta curve has stabilized with a clear peak at 8 (R²=2.45% vs 2.40% at 6 and 2.33% at 10/12); move to Stage 2 local refinement by testing max_features=88 with the winning forced_delta_count=8 to see if expanding the feature budget captures additional signal just below the top-80 threshold.
payload.planner_reasoning: Stage 1 mapping shows forced_delta_count=8 is the clear winner (holdout R²=2.447%), beating 6 (2.396%), 10 (2.331%), and 12 (2.338%). The delta-count curve is stable enough to justify Stage 2. Testing max_features=88 at the best delta count probes whether the Pearson selector's ranks 81–88 contain useful features that were excluded by the 80-feature cap, without the noise risk of a much larger jump.

payload.hypothesis: Stage 1 established forced_delta_count=8 as the peak; Stage 2 showed max_features=88 degraded R² (2.45%→2.38%), so test max_features=72 with the winning delta=8 to determine whether the optimum lies below 80 and whether tighter feature budgets reduce noise from weak marginal features.
payload.planner_reasoning: The forced-delta curve is fully mapped at max_features=80 with a clear peak at 8 (R²=2.45% vs 2.40% at 6 and 2.33% at 10/12). Moving to max_features=88 with delta=8 hurt (2.38%), suggesting the extra 8 features added noise. Testing max_features=72 with delta=8 completes the local neighborhood around the anchor and determines if pruning the weakest 8 of the top-80 features improves signal-to-noise ratio. This is the only remaining untried config in the {delta=8} × {72,80,88} Stage 2 grid.

recommendations: Start the next session near selector `pearson` with 80 features.
Investigate whether forced patterns ['*_7g', '*_7s', '*_delta_7v30g', '*_delta_7v30s'] deserve permanent quota.
